title: Argentinian Beef Asado with Dried Apricots
description: A flavorful, peppery, and gooey Argentinian beef roast featuring dried apricots. Perfect for a festive gathering.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together chopped fresh oregano, thyme, garlic, salt, and crushed black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the flank steak, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the flank steak for 2-3 minutes per side, or until a nice crust forms.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 15 minutes.
After 15 minutes, reduce the oven temperature to 300°F (150°C) and continue roasting for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 130°F (54°C) for medium-rare.
While the steak is roasting, prepare the chimichurri sauce according to the package instructions or your own recipe.
Remove the steak from the oven and let it rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
Serve the sliced steak with a spoonful of chimichurri sauce, garnished with chopped fresh rosemary and sliced red bell peppers.
Optional: drizzle with red wine vinegar for added flavor.
